name: One Step No-Machine Fresh Strawberry Ice Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine sugar and corn syrup in a small bowl.
2. Set aside.
3. Chill a medium bowl and pour in the heavy cream.
4. Using an electric mixer on Low speed, beat the cream until it holds a soft shape.
5. Cover the cream and refrigerate.
6. Wash and hull the berries.
7. Put a handful into a blender and blend, adding more through the top to make 1 1/2 cups puree.
8. Beat the sugar syrup vigorously with a spoon.
9. In a large mixing bowl, beat egg whites until soft peaks form.
10. At medium speed, gradually add the sugar syrup, beating on high until a shiny meringue holds stiff peaks.
11. Gently, but firmly, fold in the strawberry puree.
12. Next, gently fold in the beaten cream.
13. Pour into a 1/2 gallon lidded plastic container and freeze overnight.
14. Keeps for 6-8 months in freezer.
Variation 1: Peach Ice Cream
- Replace strawberries with 2 cups fresh, peeled peaches, cut into small chunks.
- Use 1 tablespoon less sugar.
- Nectarines can also be used.
Variation 2: Black Cherry Ice Cream
- Replace strawberries with 2 cups fresh, pitted ripe black cherries.
Variation 3: Blueberry Ice Cream
- Use 2 cups well-washed fresh blueberries for strawberries.
- Raspberries or blackberries can also be used.
Variation 4: Honey Apricot Ice Cream
- Replace strawberries with 2 cups fresh, peeled, pitted apricots, cut into small chunks.
- Reduce sugar/corn syrup to 1/4 cup each and add 2 tablespoons honey.
- In any recipe, you can replace half of the sugar with honey.
